# Current Bahamas — Compliance Checklist: Construction & Contractors
# Version 1.0 | April 2026
# For: General contractors, fit-out firms, project managers, developers
# Derived from master checklist — contractor-specific items added

---

## Client: ___________________________  Code: ________
## Business type: _____________  Staff: ____  Island: ________________
## Checklist prepared: _______________  By: ___________

---

## SECTION 1: BUSINESS LICENCE

| Item | Status | Notes |
|------|--------|-------|
| Business licence current and correct category (Contractor / Construction) | [ ] | |
| Sub-contractor activity covered | [ ] | |
| All active project locations covered by licence | [ ] | |
| Renewal due date in Airtable | [ ] | |
| Financial statements ready if turnover >$250K | [ ] | Common for active contractors |

---

## SECTION 2: VAT

| Item | Status | Notes |
|------|--------|-------|
| VAT registered? | [ ] Yes [ ] No | High-volume contractors almost always required to register |
| VAT applies to: labour, materials, project invoices | [ ] | Confirm applicable treatment with accountant |
| Last VAT return current | [ ] | Period: ________ Filed: ________ |
| Project-based revenue documented per VAT period | [ ] | |

---

## SECTION 3: NIB

| Item | Status | Notes |
|------|--------|-------|
| NIB employer number confirmed | [ ] | |
| Project-based staff fluctuations documented | [ ] | Headcount changes project to project |
| Sub-contractors vs employees — NIB treatment confirmed | [ ] | Sub-contractors may still trigger NIB obligations |
| Last NIB payment current | [ ] | Period: ________ Paid: ________ |
| Any arrears (common during slow project periods) | [ ] | |

---

## SECTION 4: TAX CLEARANCE CERTIFICATE (TCC) — CRITICAL FOR CONTRACTORS

| Item | Status | Notes |
|------|--------|-------|
| Current TCC on file | [ ] | |
| **TCC is required for ALL government contracts and tenders** | [ ] | This is the #1 compliance item for contractors |
| Upcoming government tenders identified | [ ] | List: |
| TCC expiry in Airtable — 30-day reminder active | [ ] | |
| Any outstanding IRD, NIB, or VAT balances (block TCC) | [ ] | Detail: |
| TCC readiness: all filings current | [ ] Ready [ ] Gap-fill needed [ ] Blocker | |

**Contractor note:** A lapsed TCC disqualifies a contractor from government work. Maintain year-round — do not apply reactively.

---

## SECTION 5: IMPORT — MATERIALS AND EQUIPMENT

| Item | Status | Notes |
|------|--------|-------|
| Does the business import materials or equipment? | [ ] Yes [ ] No | |
| Types: steel, timber, electrical, plumbing, heavy equipment | [ ] | |
| Current customs broker documented | [ ] | Name: |
| Agriculture permits required for any imported goods | [ ] | Rare for construction — confirm |
| **Phase 2 service interest:** import readiness desk | [ ] | Flag for Phase 2 activation |

---

## SECTION 6: EXEMPTIONS — CONTRACTOR SPECIFIC

| Item | Status | Notes |
|------|--------|-------|
| FIDEA eligibility (working on Family Island projects) | [ ] | |
| Capital equipment exemptions (heavy plant, vehicles, tools) | [ ] | |
| Client-side exemptions that affect contractor — SERZ/EEZ projects | [ ] | Note: client's exemption, but contractor needs to understand scope |
| Any planned equipment purchase >$10K in next 12 months | [ ] | Trigger for exemption packaging review |

---

## SECTION 7: ADDITIONAL CONTRACTOR ITEMS

| Item | Status | Notes |
|------|--------|-------|
| Work permits for foreign trades workers | [ ] | Phase 3 — log count and expiry dates |
| Vehicle / equipment licences (trucks, loaders, cranes) | [ ] | |
| Trade-specific licences (electrical, plumbing — if applicable) | [ ] | |
